item.title: Alpac Capital Agrees to Buy Adria News Network From United Group
item.description: Alpac Capital agreed to buy Adria News Network, which has news assets in Bosnia, Croatia, Montenegro, Serbia and Slovenia, from United Group BV, according to a statement released Friday.

item.title: Fried Chicken Cutlet Chain Zhengxin Food Is Said to Consider Hong Kong IPO
item.description: Shanghai Zhengxin Food Group Co., a chain specializing in fried chicken cutlets, is exploring an initial public offering in Hong Kong that may raise about $300 million, people with knowledge of the matter said.

item.title: Jupiter Fund Taps Europe’s AI Energy Boom to Beat 92% of Peers
item.description: Homing in on stocks at the heart of Europe’s electrification push has helped a Jupiter Asset Management fund team outperform 92% of its peers this year.

item.title: Billionaire Vincent Tan Said to Mull Selling Prudential Malaysia Stake
item.description: Billionaire Vincent Tan is considering selling his 30% stake in Prudential Assurance Malaysia Bhd., people with knowledge of the matter said, after already offloading a 19% holding earlier this year.

item.title: India Cuts Monsoon Forecast to 90% of Normal in Risk to Crops
item.description: India lowered its forecast for this year’s monsoon, a blow to farmers who are already contending with higher input costs due to the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Russia Heads for Showdown With Armenia Over European Ambitions
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin will join a summit of the Moscow-led Eurasian Economic Union on Friday amid growing tensions with Armenia over its shift toward Europe.

item.title: Aggressive Asia Hikes Show Few Signs of Stabilizing Currencies
item.description: Asian central banks are turning to ever more aggressive interest-rate hikes to shore up currencies, though there are few signs of success.

item.title: Singapore Salary Growth to be Measured in Uncertain Environment
item.description: Singapore’s wage growth is expected to moderate this year as companies adopt a measured approach with heightened geopolitical tensions and inflation risks.

item.title: Lenovo Doubles in Best Month Since 1999 on AI-Fueled Rally
item.description: Lenovo Group Ltd. is set for its best month in more than a quarter-century, with the stock doubling in May as investor enthusiasm builds around the company’s AI-driven growth outlook.

item.title: Iron Ore Heads for Monthly Loss as Coal Spike Pressures Margins
item.description: Iron ore headed for a monthly loss as a short-lived rally triggered by a fatal steelmaking-coal mine accident in China’s Shanxi province faded, leaving investors focused on mill margins and mounting shipments.

item.title: Japan’s Two-Year Bond Yield Rises After Lukewarm Auction
item.description: Japan’s two-year government bond yield edged higher after an auction of the tenor saw slightly weak demand as investors bet on a near-term interest rate hike by the Bank of Japan.

item.title: Australia Data-Center Boom May Keep Rates Higher, Westpac Says
item.description: The data-center boom in Australia, rivaling the country’s once-in-a-generation mining boom, could keep interest rates structurally higher than they were before the pandemic, according to Westpac Banking Corp.

item.title: Singapore’s Sea Sets Up AI Investment Team as Part of Tech Pivot
item.description: Sea Ltd. has set up a dedicated team to scout for new investments in AI, part of a broader effort to accelerate forays into the technology as it hunts for its next growth engine beyond e-commerce.

item.title: Key Themes to Watch at Asia’s Biggest AI Tech Show
item.description: Nvidia Corp.’s Jensen Huang leads a parade of AI computing leaders in Taiwan for Asia’s biggest technology showcase, Computex. Over a jam-packed week, the hardware linchpins of the post-ChatGPT era will debate the most pressing issues facing their industry, from growing bottlenecks in the supply of essential components like memory chips to the rise of challengers to Nvidia at the apex of the semiconductor hierarchy.

item.title: India Steps Up Stake Sales as Iran War Strains Finances
item.description: The asset-sale push comes even as India’s equity capital market activity has slowed over the past two months.

item.title: Industrial Metals Set for Best Month Since January on Iran Truce
item.description: Industrial metals headed for their best monthly gain since January after the US and Iran reached a tentative deal to extend their ceasefire by 60 days.

item.title: Congo Defends Ebola Response as WHO Warns Contact Tracing Is Failing
item.description: Congo’s health minister pushed back against claims the country’s Ebola outbreak was spiraling out of control, even as World Health Organization officials warned insecurity and population displacement were preventing responders from tracing most suspected contacts.

item.title: Australia Secures First Cargo Ship for Strategic Maritime Fleet
item.description: Australia has secured the first vessel for its long-awaited maritime strategic fleet program, designed to help the country respond to supply-chain disruptions such as those caused by the war in the Middle East, as well as natural disasters and other crises.

item.title: China Export Prices Climb Most in Three Years on Oil Shock
item.description: China’s export prices rose at the sharpest pace in three years, as a global oil shock filters through the world’s manufacturing powerhouse and an AI investment boom sends chip prices soaring.

item.title: New Zealand Firms Slightly Less Gloomy as Costs Remain Elevated
item.description: New Zealand business sentiment recovered slightly in May, but remains near three-year lows as surging fuel prices slow economic growth, damping customer demand and eroding profits.

item.title: SpaceX Lowers IPO Valuation Target to at Least $1.8 Trillion
item.description: SpaceX is currently targeting a valuation of at least $1.8 trillion in its initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter, as Elon Musk’s rocket and artificial intelligence company nears its debut.

item.title: Credit Agricole Taps Japan Samurai Market at Wider Spread
item.description: Credit Agricole SA set terms for ¥106.5 billion ($670 million) of samurai bonds, offering wider spreads than comparable domestic corporate debt amid rising benchmark yen rates.

item.title: Japan Banks See Hybrid Bond Boom to Fund Regulatory Capital
item.description: Japan’s major banks are set for their busiest fiscal year in over a decade for issuing a special category of bonds that will finance higher capital requirements.

item.title: Japan Can Act on Currency If There’s Volatility, Katayama Says
item.description: Japanese Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama reiterated that authorities can step into the foreign exchange market if there’s volatility or evidence of speculative moves, in comments ahead of data expected to confirm authorities intervened at some point during the past month.

item.title: Dollar’s Monthly Rise Leaves Strategists Wary of Further Gains
item.description: This month’s rally in the dollar, as traders priced in the prospect of higher US interest rates, is leaving Wall Street strategists wary of further gains.

item.title: Samsung, SK Rally Sparks Forced Selling as Funds Hit Limits
item.description: A blistering rally in Samsung Electronics Co. and SK Hynix Inc. has turned into an unexpected headache for some funds, whose positions have grown so large they’re now forced to sell.

item.title: Samsung Takes Lead in Shipping Top-End AI Memory Chip Samples
item.description: Samsung Electronics Co. has begun shipping samples of the industry’s most advanced memory to customers, taking an early lead in a race to supply the essential components for AI accelerators made by the likes of Nvidia Corp.

item.title: Tokyo Inflation Slowdown Unlikely to Derail BOJ Rate Hike
item.description: Tokyo’s key inflation gauge unexpectedly cooled to the slowest pace in four years, complicating the messaging while not necessarily derailing an expected interest-rate hike from the Bank of Japan as early as next month.

item.title: Gold Holds Gain as US-Iran Truce Hopes Calm Inflation Fears
item.description: Gold held a gain after reports that the US and Iran had reached a tentative deal to extend a ceasefire and work toward an agreement to end the Middle East war eased inflation concerns.

item.title: Tech Frenzy Sparks Shake-Up at China’s Troubled Consumer Funds
item.description: China’s battered consumer-focused funds are showing signs of pivoting to tech as a prolonged demand slump forces even the sector’s most committed backers to rethink their strategy.
